q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: The sink is Template(...).render(), an in-memory string manipulation with no network I/O, shell execution, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The rendered template body is returned as HTMLResponse(body), so the unescaped user input is present in the return value.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' with exprs ['HTMLResponse(body)'], indicating the result is directly accessible in memory on return.
